LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CHARLES LI'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT OF FU IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN 20 DAYS AS TO FIRST THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD THE FACTS THAT ARE TRUE, THE MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE AS TO THOSE FACTS, AND HOW FU WAS INDUCED TO ACT AS A CONSEQUENCE OF THE MISREPRESENTATIONS. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD THE FACTS THAT WERE CONCEALED. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD THAT THERE WAS A PROMISE MADE BY LI WITHOUT AN INTENT TO PERFORM, THAT FU RELIED ON THE PROMISE AND AS A CONSEQUENCE WAS DAMAGED. FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT LI OWED FU A DUTY OF CARE, THAT LI BREACHED THE DUTY, THEREBY INJURING FU. FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR THE CLAIM OF EXTORTION. OVERRULED AS TO SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. FU ALLEGES A CONTRACT, LI'S BREACH BY FAILING TO MAKE REASONABLE COLLECTION EFFORTS THEREBY CAUSING FU DAMAGE. 20 DAYS TO ANSWER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/AL) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CHARLES LI'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCE. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: RE INITIAL MISREPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD HOW LI'S REPRESENTATION WAS FALSE AT TIME MADE. CONCEALMENT CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO PLEAD HOW LI CONCEALED MATERIAL FACTS AT TIME ASSIGNMENT AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED. PROMISE WITHOUT INTENT TO PERFORM CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD WHETHER LI INTENDED TO PERFORM AT TIME ASSIGNMENT AGREEMENT WAS ENTERED. NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. INTENTIONAL TORT CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Cross-Defendant Charles Li Demurrer To Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-04-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Menache of Mousalam & Kim, 650-494-7700, appearing for cross defendant and moving party
Tony Fu, 867-5973, cross-complainant and responding party appearing in propria persona

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Cross-Defendant Charles Li's Demurrer To Cross-Complaint OF FU IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN 20 DAYS AS TO FIRST THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD THE FACTS THAT ARE TRUE, THE MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE AS TO THOSE FACTS, AND HOW FU WAS INDUCED TO ACT AS A CONSEQUENCE OF THE MISREPRESENTATIONS. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD THE FACTS THAT WERE CONCEALED. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD THAT THERE WAS A PROMISE MADE BY LI WITHOUT AN INTENT TO PERFORM, THAT FU RELIED ON THE PROMISE AND AS A CONSEQUENCE WAS DAMAGED. FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT LI OWED FU A DUTY OF CARE, THAT LI BREACHED THE DUTY, THEREBY INJURING FU. FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR THE CLAIM OF EXTORTION. OVERRULED AS TO SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. FU ALLEGES A CONTRACT, LI'S BREACH BY FAILING TO MAKE REASONABLE COLLECTION EFFORTS THEREBY CAUSING FU DAMAGE. 20 DAYS TO ANSWER. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AL) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Menache of Mousalam & Kim, 650-494-7700, appearing for plaintiff and responding party
Tony Fu, 867-5973, defendant and moving party appearing in propria persona

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT TONY FU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER LI KNEW ABOUT 2002 TERMINATION AGREEMENT. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AL) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
